# Workflows ‚Äî Vars

Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## Summary

| Name | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `workflows_config` | array |  | Yes | Array of workflow definitions consumed by every build-time resolver in this module (parts 4, 12, 13, 15). Host app supplies via the entry's `vars` block, e.g. `vars: { _ref: workflow_config/workflows.yaml }`. Each entry is a workflow object with `type`, `entity_collection`, `entity_ref_key` (the event-references key for the workflow's entity, e.g. `lead_ids` ‚Äî required), `starting_actions`, `action_groups`, and `actions` (see workflows-module-concept/action-authoring/spec.md). Workflows, actions, and action groups accept an optional `title:`; when omitted it is derived from the slug (`type`/`id`) by the title humanizer ‚Äî splits on `-`/`_`/camelCase, applies Title Case, lowercases minor words mid-string, uppercases acronyms (base set: PO ID URL API CRM SLA KPI VAT PDF CSV FAQ KYC RFQ; extended by `title_acronyms`). Set `title:` only when the default is wrong.  |
| `app_name` | string |  | Yes | The host app's deployment name. Serves three roles: (1) filters action access via `access.{app_name}` per action; (2) keys the engine's default log event display block (matching the events module's display_key projection); and (3) keys the per-app action display the engine writes onto each action doc ‚Äî display surfaces read `action.{app_name}.message` and `action.{app_name}.links` for the current app's slug.  |
| `entities` | object |  | Yes | Map keyed by workflow `entity_collection` ‚Üí `{ page_id, id_query_key, title }`. The module deep-links back into host-app entity pages (workflow-overview back-link, workflow-header entity-kind chrome) using these entries. - `page_id` ‚Äî host-app page id rendering the entity. - `id_query_key` ‚Äî URL query string key the entity page expects for its primary id (commonly `_id`). - `title` ‚Äî singular human-readable entity-kind label (e.g. "Lead", "Ticket"). Per-key shape is not statically validated by Lowdefy; part 4's `makeWorkflowsConfig` validator confirms every `entity_collection` referenced in `workflows_config` has a matching key here.  |
| `action_statuses_display` | object | `{}` |  | Per-status display overrides for the shipped action_statuses enum. Merged via `_build.object.assign` onto the shipped enum and exposed as the `action_statuses` component for UI consumption only. The engine reads the shipped enum directly from the module file ‚Äî overrides are UI-only and cannot affect engine behaviour (transition legality is owned by the per-kind FSM tables; the enum's `priority` is display-only ordering).  |
| `workflow_lifecycle_stages_display` | object | `{}` |  | Per-stage display overrides for the shipped workflow_lifecycle_stages enum. Same merge semantics as action_statuses_display: UI-only, cannot affect engine logic.  |
| `title_acronyms` | array | `[]` |  | Domain acronyms (e.g. [BOM, SKU]) merged into the module's base acronym set and applied by the title humanizer when deriving default titles from workflow/action/group slugs. A token whose lowercased form matches an entry is fully uppercased in derived titles (e.g. "upload-bom" ‚Üí "Upload BOM"). Base set ships in the module; this var extends it. Has no effect on explicitly authored `title:` values.  |
| `contacts_collection` | string | `user-contacts` |  | Collection name the timeline lookup (GetEventsTimeline) joins to resolve each event author's avatar (created.user.id ‚Üí _id, projecting profile.picture). Defaults to `user-contacts` ‚Äî the shared collection where a user IS a contact (same _id space). Set this to match your contacts collection name if the host app points the contacts module at a custom collection.  |
